name: Card
description: Cards frame dashboard content into readable editorial modules, supporting metrics, stories, approvals, filters, and activity summaries without breaking the calm premium tone of the system.
summary: Use cards as the default content surface for grouped information. They should feel soft and intentional, with restrained borders, rounded corners, and enough spacing to let charts, numbers, and prose breathe.

variants.name: standard
variants.useCase: Most dashboard modules such as KPI summaries, notes, and review queues.

variants.name: highlighted
variants.useCase: Priority insights, featured reports, or selected account plans.

variants.name: interactive
variants.useCase: Clickable cards used for navigation, drill-down, or selection.

contentGuidelines: Use a clear title and one supporting sentence before introducing complex data.
Keep KPI cards focused on one primary number and one explanatory detail.
Avoid stacking more than two competing actions inside the same card footer.

layoutGuidelines.padding: Use multiple spacing units to preserve an airy editorial rhythm.
layoutGuidelines.headerStructure: Place the title before actions whenever the card communicates analysis or progress.
layoutGuidelines.density: Favor medium density; dense packing undermines the premium dashboard character.

accessibility.landmarks: Do not assign landmark roles unless the card meaningfully groups a region or contains actionable controls.
accessibility.interactiveVariant: When the whole card is clickable, preserve a visible focus state and a clear action label.
accessibility.readingOrder: Titles, values, annotations, and actions should follow a logical top-to-bottom sequence.

compositionNotes: Cards pair naturally with buttons, inputs, tables, and chart panels because they share the same surface, border, and radius language.
Highlighted cards should use accent sparingly so they stand out without turning the dashboard into a patchwork of saturated panels.
Shadow remains intentionally subtle; hierarchy should come from spacing, typography, and layout before elevation.
